old_claim.note: Hospital Information Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from ultrasound_medical_imaging to hospital_information_systems; HIS belongs to clinical documentation and workflow rather than the ultrasound modality itself.

source_shape.source_locator: BMUS history of ultrasound from early medical ultrasonics through clinical scanner development.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source explains ultrasound development through high-frequency sound, clinical scanning, electronics, and transducers, not hospital information systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This rejects a direct HIS dependency because the medical ultrasound modality emerged before digital hospital information-system workflows and does not require them for sound acquisition.

ontology_before: Ultrasound included hospital information systems as a direct prerequisite, mixing modality physics with clinical data infrastructure.
ontology_after: Ultrasound is scoped to acoustic imaging technology and clinical interpretation; hospital information systems remain separate digital-health infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital information systems no longer appear as a direct ultrasound prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a modern ultrasound-reporting workflow that explicitly requires HIS integration.
A source showed ultrasound acquisition physically requires a hospital information system.
Removing the edge also removed all clinical medicine context from ultrasound.
short_reason: Hospital information systems are workflow infrastructure, not a prerequisite for ultrasound imaging.
